    <h1 {...{
      "id": "add-on-architecture-guide"
    }}>{`Add-on Architecture Guide`}</h1>
    <p>{`Learn about the dual-runtime architecture, communication patterns, and development workflow essentials for building Adobe Express add-ons.`}</p>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "overview"
    }}>{`Overview`}</h2>
    <p>{`Add-ons run as iframes within Adobe Express, with isolated runtime environments communicating through a secure proxy layer. This guide explains add-on's dual-runtime architecture, cross-runtime communication, SDK usage, and development best practices for building secure and performant add-ons.`}</p>
    <InlineAlert variant="info" slots="header, text1" mdxType="InlineAlert" />
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`New to add-on terminology?`}</strong></p>
    <p>{`If you're unfamiliar with terms like "iframe runtime," "Document Sandbox SDK," or "Express Document SDK," check out the `}<a parentName="p" {...{
        "href": "../fundamentals/terminology.md"
      }}>{`Add-on Development Terminology Guide`}</a>{` for clear definitions and quick reference charts. This architecture guide assumes familiarity with those core concepts.`}</p>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "dual-runtime-architecture"
    }}>{`Dual-Runtime Architecture`}</h2>
    <p>{`Adobe Express add-ons run in `}<strong parentName="p">{`two separate JavaScript execution environments`}</strong>{` that work together:`}</p>
    <ol>
      <li parentName="ol"><strong parentName="li">{`iframe runtime`}</strong>{` - Your add-on's user interface environment (uses the Add-on UI SDK)`}</li>
      <li parentName="ol"><strong parentName="li">{`document sandbox`}</strong>{` - Secure environment for document manipulation (uses the Document Sandbox SDK)`}</li>
    </ol>
    <p><img parentName="p" {...{
        "src": "/express-add-ons-docs/assets/94edc1d03b237961a8262f854d1deafd/architecture.svg",
        "alt": "Runtime Architecture Diagram"
      }}></img></p>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "how-it-works"
    }}>{`How it works`}</h3>
    <p>{`Your add-on is bundled and loaded as an iframe within Adobe Express. Both runtime environments are isolated from each other and from the host application for security. They communicate exclusively through a proxy-based message passing system, which ensures that:`}</p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul">{`The UI can access the document through specific Add-on UI SDK methods (ie: `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`app.document.addImage()`}</inlineCode>{`) but does not directly manipulate document elements`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`The document sandbox cannot directly access the iframe's HTML DOM`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`All cross-runtime communication is type-safe and validated`}</li>
    </ul>
    <p>{`This isolation is fundamental to Adobe Express's security model, allowing third-party add-ons to extend functionality without compromising user data or application stability.`}</p>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "the-two-environments"
    }}>{`The Two Environments`}</h2>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "iframe-runtime"
    }}>{`Iframe Runtime`}</h3>
    <p>{`The browser environment where the add-on's user interface runs for users to interact with it. When a user chooses an add-on, the add-on opens in a panel on the right side of the Adobe Express UI in a secure sandboxed iframe, where permissions are controlled by a Permissions Policy and the `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`sandbox`}</inlineCode>{` attribute. See the `}<a parentName="p" {...{
        "href": "./context.md"
      }}>{`Iframe Runtime Context & Security Guide`}</a>{` for more details on the iframe runtime and its security. The iframe runtime is where the Add-on UI SDK `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`addOnUISdk`}</inlineCode>{` is imported to access the APIs for features like OAuth, media import, rendition creation, and more.`}</p>
    <table>
      <thead parentName="table">
        <tr parentName="thead">
          <th parentName="tr" {...{
            "align": null
          }}>{`Attribute`}</th>
          <th parentName="tr" {...{
            "align": null
          }}>{`Details`}</th>
        </tr>
      </thead>
      <tbody parentName="table">
        <tr parentName="tbody">
          <td parentName="tr" {...{
            "align": null
          }}><strong parentName="td">{`File`}</strong></td>
          <td parentName="tr" {...{
            "align": null
          }}><inlineCode parentName="td">{`index.js`}</inlineCode>{` or `}<inlineCode parentName="td">{`index.html`}</inlineCode></td>
        </tr>
        <tr parentName="tbody">
          <td parentName="tr" {...{
            "align": null
          }}><strong parentName="td">{`SDKs Used`}</strong></td>
          <td parentName="tr" {...{
            "align": null
          }}>{`Add-on UI SDK`}</td>
        </tr>
        <tr parentName="tbody">
          <td parentName="tr" {...{
            "align": null
          }}><strong parentName="td">{`Add Content`}</strong></td>
          <td parentName="tr" {...{
            "align": null
          }}><strong parentName="td">{`UI components`}</strong>{`: Render HTML, CSS, JavaScript frameworks`}<br /><strong parentName="td">{`Browser access`}</strong>{`: DOM manipulation, fetch, localStorage, etc.`}<br /><strong parentName="td">{`Add-on UI SDK features`}</strong>{` (via `}<inlineCode parentName="td">{`addOnUISdk`}</inlineCode>{`):`}<br />{`• `}<inlineCode parentName="td">{`app.document.addImage()`}</inlineCode>{` - Import media`}<br />{`• `}<inlineCode parentName="td">{`app.document.createRenditions()`}</inlineCode>{` - Export document`}<br />{`• `}<inlineCode parentName="td">{`app.showModalDialog()`}</inlineCode>{` - Display dialogs`}<br />{`• `}<inlineCode parentName="td">{`app.oauth`}</inlineCode>{` - Auth flows`}<br />{`• `}<inlineCode parentName="td">{`addOnUISdk.instance.clientStorage`}</inlineCode>{` - Persistent storage`}<br /><strong parentName="td">{`Communication`}</strong>{`: Use `}<inlineCode parentName="td">{`runtime.apiProxy()`}</inlineCode>{` to call sandbox functions or `}<inlineCode parentName="td">{`runtime.exposeApi()`}</inlineCode>{` to expose functions to the document sandbox`}</td>
        </tr>
      </tbody>
    </table>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`Import Pattern:`}</strong></p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-js"
      }}>{`import addOnUISdk from "https://express.adobe.com/static/add-on-sdk/sdk.js";

// Always wait for SDK to be ready
addOnUISdk.ready.then(() => {
  const { runtime } = addOnUISdk.instance;
  const { app } = addOnUISdk;

  // runtime - for communication with document sandbox
  // app - for UI SDK features (dialogs, OAuth, renditions, etc.)
});
`}</code></pre>
    <InlineAlert slots="header, text1, text2, text3, text4" variant="success" mdxType="InlineAlert" />
    <p>{`When do I need document sandbox communication?`}</p>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`✅ YES`}</strong>{` - You need `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`runtime.apiProxy()`}</inlineCode>{` to communicate with the document sandbox if:`}</p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Creating/modifying document elements (text, shapes, etc.)`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Reading document properties not available in UI SDK`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Performing complex document operations`}</li>
    </ul>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`❌ NO`}</strong>{` - You don't need it if:`}</p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Only using Add-on UI SDK features (e.g., `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`app.document.addImage()`}</inlineCode>{`, `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`app.document.createRenditions()`}</inlineCode>{`)`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Building a pure UI add-on (settings, external integrations)`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Only displaying information fetched from external APIs`}</li>
    </ul>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "document-sandbox"
    }}>{`Document Sandbox`}</h3>
    <p>{`The secure isolated environment for document manipulation with limited browser APIs but direct document access. The document sandbox is where the Document Sandbox SDKs are used to access the APIs for features like document manipulation, document properties, and more.`}</p>
    <table>
      <thead parentName="table">
        <tr parentName="thead">
          <th parentName="tr" {...{
            "align": null
          }}>{`Attribute`}</th>
          <th parentName="tr" {...{
            "align": null
          }}>{`Details`}</th>
        </tr>
      </thead>
      <tbody parentName="table">
        <tr parentName="tbody">
          <td parentName="tr" {...{
            "align": null
          }}><strong parentName="td">{`File`}</strong></td>
          <td parentName="tr" {...{
            "align": null
          }}><inlineCode parentName="td">{`code.js`}</inlineCode></td>
        </tr>
        <tr parentName="tbody">
          <td parentName="tr" {...{
            "align": null
          }}><strong parentName="td">{`SDKs Used`}</strong></td>
          <td parentName="tr" {...{
            "align": null
          }}>{`Document Sandbox SDK (for communication) + Express Document SDK (for Document APIs)`}</td>
        </tr>
        <tr parentName="tbody">
          <td parentName="tr" {...{
            "align": null
          }}><strong parentName="td">{`Capabilities`}</strong></td>
          <td parentName="tr" {...{
            "align": null
          }}><strong parentName="td">{`Direct document manipulation`}</strong>{`: Create/modify shapes, text, images using `}<inlineCode parentName="td">{`editor`}</inlineCode><br /><strong parentName="td">{`Scenegraph access`}</strong>{`: Read and traverse the document structure`}<br /><strong parentName="td">{`Express Document SDK features`}</strong>{`:`}<br />{`• `}<inlineCode parentName="td">{`editor.createRectangle()`}</inlineCode>{`, `}<inlineCode parentName="td">{`editor.createText()`}</inlineCode>{` - Create content`}<br />{`• `}<inlineCode parentName="td">{`editor.context.selection`}</inlineCode>{` - Access selected elements`}<br />{`• `}<inlineCode parentName="td">{`editor.context.insertionParent`}</inlineCode>{` - Add content to document`}<br />{`• `}<inlineCode parentName="td">{`colorUtils`}</inlineCode>{` - Create and convert colors`}<br />{`• `}<inlineCode parentName="td">{`fonts`}</inlineCode>{` - Manage and load fonts`}<br />{`• `}<inlineCode parentName="td">{`viewport`}</inlineCode>{` - Control canvas navigation`}<br /><strong parentName="td">{`Limited Web APIs`}</strong>{`: Only `}<inlineCode parentName="td">{`console`}</inlineCode>{` and `}<inlineCode parentName="td">{`Blob`}</inlineCode>{` (see the `}<a parentName="td" {...{
              "href": "../../../references/document-sandbox/web/index.md"
            }}>{`Web APIs Reference`}</a>{`)`}<br /><strong parentName="td">{`Communication`}</strong>{`: Use `}<inlineCode parentName="td">{`runtime.exposeApi()`}</inlineCode>{` to expose functions to the iframe runtime or `}<inlineCode parentName="td">{`runtime.apiProxy()`}</inlineCode>{` to call UI functions`}</td>
        </tr>
      </tbody>
    </table>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`Import Patterns:`}</strong></p>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`Option 1: Communication Only`}</strong></p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-js"
      }}>{`import addOnSandboxSdk from "add-on-sdk-document-sandbox";
const { runtime } = addOnSandboxSdk.instance;

runtime.exposeApi({
  processData: function(data) {
    // Process data without touching document
    return data.map(item => item.toUpperCase());
  }
});
`}</code></pre>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`Option 2: Communication + Document Manipulation (Most Common)`}</strong></p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-js"
      }}>{`import addOnSandboxSdk from "add-on-sdk-document-sandbox";
import { editor, colorUtils, constants, fonts, viewport } from "express-document-sdk";

const { runtime } = addOnSandboxSdk.instance;

runtime.exposeApi({
  createShape: function() {
    const rectangle = editor.createRectangle();
    rectangle.width = 100;
    rectangle.height = 100;
    editor.context.insertionParent.children.append(rectangle);
  }
});
`}</code></pre>
    <InlineAlert slots="header, text1, text2, text3, text4" variant="success" mdxType="InlineAlert" />
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`Which SDKs do I need to import?`}</strong></p>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`Document Sandbox SDK (`}<inlineCode parentName="strong">{`addOnSandboxSdk`}</inlineCode>{`):`}</strong></p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul">{`✅ YES if your `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`code.js`}</inlineCode>{` needs to communicate with the iframe runtime`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`✅ YES if iframe runtime triggers document operations`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`❌ NO if you don't have a `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`documentSandbox`}</inlineCode>{` entry in your manifest`}</li>
    </ul>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`Express Document SDK (`}<inlineCode parentName="strong">{`express-document-sdk`}</inlineCode>{`):`}</strong></p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul">{`✅ YES if creating/modifying document content`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`✅ YES if accessing document properties`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`❌ NO if only processing data or communicating`}</li>
    </ul>
    <InlineAlert variant="info" slots="header, text1" mdxType="InlineAlert" />
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`Code Playground Note`}</strong></p>
    <p>{`The Adobe Express `}<a parentName="p" {...{
        "href": "../../getting_started/code-playground.md"
      }}>{`Code Playground`}</a>{` has special behavior where the `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`express-document-sdk`}</inlineCode>{` is automatically injected in Script mode. In production add-ons, you must always use explicit import statements as shown above.`}</p>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`What's Available Without Import:`}</strong></p>
    <p>{`The document sandbox automatically provides limited Web APIs and standard JavaScript built-ins. For complete details, see `}<a parentName="p" {...{
        "href": "../../../references/document-sandbox/web/index.md"
      }}>{`Web APIs Reference`}</a>{`.`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-js"
      }}>{`// Console APIs - for debugging
console.log("Debugging output");
console.error("Error logging");
console.warn("Warnings");
console.assert(condition, "Assertion message");

// Blob interface - for binary data handling
const blob = new Blob(['data'], { type: 'text/plain' });
blob.text().then(text => console.log(text));

// Standard JavaScript built-ins - available globally
JSON.parse('{"key": "value"}');
Math.floor(3.7);
Date.now();
Object.keys({a: 1});
Array.from([1, 2, 3]);
String.prototype.toUpperCase.call('hello');
// ... and other standard JavaScript globals
`}</code></pre>
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "environment-characteristics"
    }}>{`Environment Characteristics`}</h4>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li">{`Isolated JavaScript context`}</strong>{`: Secure sandbox execution`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li">{`Limited browser APIs`}</strong>{`: Only essential APIs like `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`console`}</inlineCode>{` and `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`Blob`}</inlineCode></li>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li">{`Performance`}</strong>{`: Slower than iframe runtime but secure`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li">{`No HTML DOM access`}</strong>{`: Must communicate through iframe runtime for UI updates`}</li>
    </ul>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`Debugging Tips:`}</strong></p>
    <p>{`The console object is available in the document sandbox to allow you to log messages to the console. You can use it to debug your code by logging variables, objects, and other information to the console. For example:`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-js"
      }}>{`// View variable values
console.log("Variable:", myVariable);

// Object inspection - serialize to see structure
console.log("Object:", JSON.stringify(myObject, null, 2));

// Error tracking
try {
    const result = editor.createText("Hello");
    console.log("Success:", result);
} catch (error) {
    console.error("Failed:", error.message);
}

// Performance debugging
const start = performance.now();
// ... your code ...
console.log(\`Took \${performance.now() - start}ms\`);
`}</code></pre>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "the-runtime-object"
    }}>{`The Runtime Object`}</h3>
    <p>{`The `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`runtime`}</inlineCode>{` object provides `}<strong parentName="p">{`communication APIs`}</strong>{` that enable the two environments to talk to each other. Each environment has its own runtime object for secure message passing.`}</p>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`Where to Access:`}</strong></p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li">{`iframe runtime`}</strong>{`: `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`addOnUISdk.instance.runtime`}</inlineCode>{` (from the Add-on UI SDK)`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li">{`document sandbox`}</strong>{`: `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`addOnSandboxSdk.instance.runtime`}</inlineCode>{` (from the Document Sandbox SDK)`}</li>
    </ul>
    <p>{`Think of it like a phone - where each side has their own phone with two key methods:`}</p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li"><inlineCode parentName="strong">{`exposeApi()`}</inlineCode></strong>{` - Makes your functions callable from the other side (like publishing your phone number)`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li"><inlineCode parentName="strong">{`apiProxy()`}</inlineCode></strong>{` - Gets a proxy to call functions on the other side (like dialing the other phone)`}</li>
    </ul>
    <p>{`This abstraction ensures:`}</p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Type-safe cross-runtime communication`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Security boundaries are maintained`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Both environments can initiate communication`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`No direct access between environments`}</li>
    </ul>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "communication-flow"
    }}>{`Communication Flow`}</h2>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "from-iframe-runtime-to-document-sandbox"
    }}>{`From Iframe Runtime to Document Sandbox`}</h3>
    <p>{`To manipulate the document from your UI, use the following pattern:`}</p>
    <CodeBlock slots="heading, code" repeat="2" languages="JavaScript, JavaScript" mdxType="CodeBlock" />
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "iframe-runtime-uiindexjs"
    }}>{`Iframe Runtime (ui/index.js)`}</h4>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-js"
      }}>{`/**
 * ENVIRONMENT: Iframe Runtime (UI)
 * FILE: src/ui/index.js
 * PURPOSE: Handle user interactions and trigger document operations
 * SDK: Add-on UI SDK
 */
import addOnUISdk, { RuntimeType } from "https://express.adobe.com/static/add-on-sdk/sdk.js";

// Wait for SDK to be ready before accessing runtime
addOnUISdk.ready.then(async () => {
  const { runtime } = addOnUISdk.instance;

  // Button click handler - responds to user action
  document.getElementById("createText").addEventListener("click", async () => {
    // Step 1: Get a proxy to call document sandbox functions
    const sandboxProxy = await runtime.apiProxy(RuntimeType.documentSandbox);

    // Step 2: Call the function exposed in code.js
    await sandboxProxy.createTextElement("Hello World!");
  });
});
`}</code></pre>
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "document-sandbox-sandboxcodejs"
    }}>{`Document Sandbox (sandbox/code.js)`}</h4>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-js"
      }}>{`/**
 * ENVIRONMENT: Document Sandbox (Isolated)
 * FILE: src/sandbox/code.js
 * PURPOSE: Perform document manipulation operations
 * SDKs: Document Sandbox SDK (communication) + Express Document SDK (document APIs)
 */
import addOnSandboxSdk from "add-on-sdk-document-sandbox";  // For communication
import { editor } from "express-document-sdk";              // For document manipulation

const { runtime } = addOnSandboxSdk.instance;

// Expose functions that Iframe Runtime can call
runtime.exposeApi({
  createTextElement: function(text) {
    const textNode = editor.createText(text);
    editor.context.insertionParent.children.append(textNode);
  }
});
`}</code></pre>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "from-document-sandbox-to-iframe-runtime"
    }}>{`From Document Sandbox to Iframe Runtime`}</h3>
    <p>{`To update the UI from the document sandbox, use the following pattern:`}</p>
    <CodeBlock slots="heading, code" repeat="2" languages="JavaScript, JavaScript" mdxType="CodeBlock" />
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "document-sandbox-sandboxcodejs-1"
    }}>{`Document Sandbox (sandbox/code.js)`}</h4>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-js"
      }}>{`/**
 * ENVIRONMENT: Document Sandbox (Isolated)
 * FILE: src/sandbox/code.js
 * PURPOSE: Process document and send updates to UI
 * SDK: Document Sandbox SDK (for communication back to UI)
 */

import addOnSandboxSdk, { RuntimeType } from "add-on-sdk-document-sandbox";

const { runtime } = addOnSandboxSdk.instance;

async function processDocument() {
  // Step 1: Get a proxy to call UI functions
  const uiProxy = await runtime.apiProxy(RuntimeType.panel);

  // Step 2: Update UI with progress
  await uiProxy.updateProgress(50);

  // Step 3: Perform document manipulation here...

  // Step 4: Notify UI when complete
  await uiProxy.updateProgress(100);
}
`}</code></pre>
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "iframe-runtime-uiindexjs-1"
    }}>{`Iframe Runtime (ui/index.js)`}</h4>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-js"
      }}>{`/**
 * ENVIRONMENT: Iframe Runtime (UI)
 * FILE: src/ui/index.js
 * PURPOSE: Expose UI update functions that document sandbox can call
 * SDK: Add-on UI SDK
 */
import addOnUISdk from "https://express.adobe.com/static/add-on-sdk/sdk.js";

addOnUISdk.ready.then(() => {
  const { runtime } = addOnUISdk.instance;

  // Expose functions that document sandbox can call
  runtime.exposeApi({
    updateProgress: function(percentage) {
      // Update the DOM directly (iframe has standard Web APIs)
      const progressBar = document.getElementById("progress");
      progressBar.style.width = percentage + "%";
    }
  });
});
`}</code></pre>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "manifest-configuration"
    }}>{`Manifest Configuration`}</h3>
    <p>{`Your add-on's `}<a parentName="p" {...{
        "href": "../../../references/manifest/index.md"
      }}><inlineCode parentName="a">{`manifest.json`}</inlineCode></a>{` is where you enable communication between the iframe runtime and the document sandbox, by specifying the `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`documentSandbox`}</inlineCode>{` entrypoint and the code file to execute in the document sandbox.`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-json"
      }}>{`/**
 * FILE: manifest.json
 * PURPOSE: Define add-on structure and entrypoints
 */
{
  "entryPoints": [
    {
      "type": "panel",              // Creates a panel in the Adobe Express UI
      "id": "panel1",                // Unique panel identifier
      "main": "index.html",          // UI entry point (iframe runtime)
      "documentSandbox": "code.js"   // Document sandbox code (optional)
    }
  ]
}
`}</code></pre>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`Communication Between Environments:`}</strong></p>
    <p>{`Use the `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`runtime.apiProxy()`}</inlineCode>{` method with `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`RuntimeType`}</inlineCode>{` constants for type-safe communication:`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-js"
      }}>{`// In iframe runtime (index.js)
import addOnUISdk, { RuntimeType } from "https://express.adobe.com/static/add-on-sdk/sdk.js";

const sandboxProxy = await runtime.apiProxy(RuntimeType.documentSandbox);
`}</code></pre>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-js"
      }}>{`// In document sandbox (code.js)
import addOnSandboxSdk, { RuntimeType } from "add-on-sdk-document-sandbox";

const { runtime } = addOnSandboxSdk.instance;
const uiProxy = await runtime.apiProxy(RuntimeType.panel);
`}</code></pre>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`How it works:`}</strong></p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li">{`From iframe runtime`}</strong>{`: Call `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`runtime.apiProxy(RuntimeType.documentSandbox)`}</inlineCode>{` to communicate with the document sandbox`}
        <ul parentName="li">
          <li parentName="ul">{`Requires `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`"documentSandbox": "code.js"`}</inlineCode>{` in your manifest`}</li>
        </ul>
      </li>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li">{`From document sandbox`}</strong>{`: Call `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`runtime.apiProxy(RuntimeType.panel)`}</inlineCode>{` to communicate with the iframe runtime`}
        <ul parentName="li">
          <li parentName="ul">{`The panel type matches `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`"type": "panel"`}</inlineCode>{` from your manifest`}</li>
        </ul>
      </li>
    </ul>
    <InlineAlert variant="info" slots="header, text1" mdxType="InlineAlert" />
    <p>{`Important Notes`}</p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul"><inlineCode parentName="li">{`"documentSandbox": "code.js"`}</inlineCode>{` in manifest enables the document sandbox runtime`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`If `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`documentSandbox`}</inlineCode>{` is omitted, only the iframe runtime runs (UI-only add-on)`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Always use `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`RuntimeType`}</inlineCode>{` constants for type safety in both environments`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Import `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`RuntimeType`}</inlineCode>{` from the appropriate SDK in each environment`}</li>
    </ul>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "common-communication-patterns"
    }}>{`Common Communication Patterns`}</h3>
    <p>{`Now that you understand the communication flow, here are the most common patterns you'll use:`}</p>
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "pattern-1-ui-triggered-document-operations"
    }}>{`Pattern 1: UI-Triggered Document Operations`}</h4>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`Most common`}</strong>{` - User clicks button in UI → Create/modify document elements.`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-js"
      }}>{`/**
 * PATTERN: UI-Triggered Document Operations
 * FLOW: User Action (iframe) → API Call → Document Change (sandbox)
 * FILES: index.js + code.js
 */

// index.js (Iframe Runtime)
import addOnUISdk, { RuntimeType } from "https://express.adobe.com/static/add-on-sdk/sdk.js";

addOnUISdk.ready.then(async () => {
  const { runtime } = addOnUISdk.instance;
  const sandboxProxy = await runtime.apiProxy(RuntimeType.documentSandbox);

  document.getElementById("createBtn").onclick = async () => {
    await sandboxProxy.createTextElement("Hello!");
  };
});

// code.js (Document Sandbox)
import addOnSandboxSdk from "add-on-sdk-document-sandbox";
import { editor } from "express-document-sdk";

const { runtime } = addOnSandboxSdk.instance;

runtime.exposeApi({
  createTextElement: function(text) {
    const textNode = editor.createText(text);
    editor.context.insertionParent.children.append(textNode);
  }
});
`}</code></pre>
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "pattern-2-document-driven-ui-updates"
    }}>{`Pattern 2: Document-Driven UI Updates`}</h4>
    <p>{`Read document properties → Display in UI.`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-js"
      }}>{`/**
 * PATTERN: Document-Driven UI Updates
 * FLOW: Document Read (sandbox) → Send Data → UI Update (iframe)
 * FILES: index.js + code.js
 */

// index.js (Iframe Runtime)
import addOnUISdk, { RuntimeType } from "https://express.adobe.com/static/add-on-sdk/sdk.js";

addOnUISdk.ready.then(async () => {
  const { runtime } = addOnUISdk.instance;
  const sandboxProxy = await runtime.apiProxy(RuntimeType.documentSandbox);

  const stats = await sandboxProxy.getDocumentStats();
  document.getElementById("stats").textContent =
    \`Pages: \${stats.pageCount}, Elements: \${stats.elementCount}\`;
});

// code.js (Document Sandbox)
import addOnSandboxSdk from "add-on-sdk-document-sandbox";
import { editor } from "express-document-sdk";

const { runtime } = addOnSandboxSdk.instance;

runtime.exposeApi({
  getDocumentStats: function() {
    return {
      pageCount: editor.documentRoot.pages.length,
      elementCount: editor.context.insertionParent.children.length
    };
  }
});
`}</code></pre>
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "pattern-3-bi-directional-communication"
    }}>{`Pattern 3: Bi-directional Communication`}</h4>
    <p>{`Document sandbox calls back to UI to show progress.`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-js"
      }}>{`/**
 * PATTERN: Bi-directional Communication
 * FLOW: UI → Sandbox → Document + Sandbox → UI (progress updates)
 * FILES: index.js + code.js
 */

// index.js (Iframe Runtime)
import addOnUISdk, { RuntimeType } from "https://express.adobe.com/static/add-on-sdk/sdk.js";

addOnUISdk.ready.then(async () => {
  const { runtime } = addOnUISdk.instance;

  // Expose progress handler
  runtime.exposeApi({
    updateProgress: (percent) => {
      document.getElementById("progress").style.width = percent + "%";
    }
  });

  const sandboxProxy = await runtime.apiProxy(RuntimeType.documentSandbox);
  await sandboxProxy.processLargeOperation();
});

// code.js (Document Sandbox)
import addOnSandboxSdk, { RuntimeType } from "add-on-sdk-document-sandbox";

const { runtime } = addOnSandboxSdk.instance;

runtime.exposeApi({
  processLargeOperation: async function() {
    const uiProxy = await runtime.apiProxy(RuntimeType.panel);

    await uiProxy.updateProgress(25);
    // ... do work ...
    await uiProxy.updateProgress(50);
    // ... more work ...
    await uiProxy.updateProgress(100);
  }
});
`}</code></pre>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "sdk-structure--import-patterns"
    }}>{`SDK Structure & Import Patterns`}</h2>
    <InlineAlert variant="warning" slots="header, text1" mdxType="InlineAlert" />
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`Important Concept`}</strong></p>
    <p>{`The Adobe Express add-on SDKs use the `}<strong parentName="p">{`singleton pattern`}</strong>{` which means objects are instantiated once and can be accessed globally. Since the objects are pre-instantiated when you import them, you never use `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`new`}</inlineCode>{` to create any new instances yourself. Understanding this pattern is essential for correct usage. The singleton approach provides reliability, consistency, efficiency, and simplicity in add-on development.`}</p>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "examples-of-sdk-singletons"
    }}>{`Examples of SDK Singletons`}</h3>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`Add-on UI SDK Singletons`}</strong></p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul"><inlineCode parentName="li">{`addOnUISdk.instance`}</inlineCode>{` - Add-on-specific features`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul"><inlineCode parentName="li">{`addOnUISdk.app`}</inlineCode>{` - Adobe Express application interface`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul"><inlineCode parentName="li">{`addOnUISdk.instance.runtime`}</inlineCode>{` - Communication between environments`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul"><inlineCode parentName="li">{`addOnUISdk.app.document`}</inlineCode>{` - Document import/export`}</li>
    </ul>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`Document Sandbox SDK Singletons`}</strong></p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul"><inlineCode parentName="li">{`addOnSandboxSdk.instance`}</inlineCode>{` - Add-on instance in sandbox`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul"><inlineCode parentName="li">{`addOnSandboxSdk.instance.runtime`}</inlineCode>{` - Communication from sandbox`}</li>
    </ul>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`Express Document SDK Singletons:`}</strong></p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul"><inlineCode parentName="li">{`editor`}</inlineCode>{` - Document editing and manipulation`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul"><inlineCode parentName="li">{`colorUtils`}</inlineCode>{` - Color creation and conversion`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul"><inlineCode parentName="li">{`constants`}</inlineCode>{` - Document constants for type safety`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul"><inlineCode parentName="li">{`fonts`}</inlineCode>{` - Font management`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul"><inlineCode parentName="li">{`viewport`}</inlineCode>{` - Viewport control`}</li>
    </ul>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`Remember:`}</strong>{` You never call `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`new`}</inlineCode>{` on any of these, they're ready to use when you import them.`}</p>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "the-complete-runtime-hierarchy"
    }}>{`The Complete Runtime Hierarchy`}</h3>
    <p>{`When a user opens your add-on, one running instance is created with all SDK singleton objects pre-instantiated and ready to use.`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-text"
      }}>{`Your Add-on Instance (when user opens your panel)
├── Iframe Runtime Environment
│   ├── addOnUISdk.instance
│   │   ├── runtime (communication APIs)
│   │   ├── clientStorage (persistent storage)
│   │   └── manifest (manifest details)
│   └── addOnUISdk.app
│       ├── ui (theme, locale, panels)
│       ├── document (import/export, renditions)
│       ├── oauth (authentication)
│       └── currentUser (user info)
└── Document Sandbox Environment (if configured)
    ├── addOnSandboxSdk.instance
    │   └── runtime (communication APIs)
    └── Express Document SDK instances
        ├── editor (document manipulation)
        ├── colorUtils (color utilities)
        ├── constants (document constants)
        ├── fonts (font management)
        └── viewport (viewport control)
`}</code></pre>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "sdk-export-patterns--naming"
    }}>{`SDK Export Patterns & Naming`}</h3>
    <p>{`The three Adobe Express add-on SDKs use different export patterns:`}</p>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`Add-on UI SDK & Document Sandbox SDK:`}</strong></p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li">{`Default export`}</strong>{` of SDK object: `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`import addOnUISdk from "https://express.adobe.com/static/add-on-sdk/sdk.js"`}</inlineCode></li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Access singletons via `}<strong parentName="li">{`properties`}</strong>{`: `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`addOnUISdk.instance`}</inlineCode>{`, `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`addOnUISdk.app`}</inlineCode></li>
    </ul>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`Express Document SDK:`}</strong></p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li">{`Named exports`}</strong>{` of singleton instances: `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`import { editor, colorUtils } from "express-document-sdk"`}</inlineCode></li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Lowercase names are the singletons you use`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Uppercase names (`}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`Editor`}</inlineCode>{`, `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`ColorUtils`}</inlineCode>{`) are the TypeScript types (see the `}<a parentName="li" {...{
          "href": "../../../references/document-sandbox/document-apis/index.md"
        }}>{`Express Document SDK Reference`}</a>{` for more details)`}</li>
    </ul>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "usage-example"
    }}>{`Usage Example`}</h3>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-javascript"
      }}>{`// Document sandbox (sandbox/code.js) - Import all singletons you need
import { editor, colorUtils, constants, fonts, viewport } from "express-document-sdk";

// Use the singletons directly
const rectangle = editor.createRectangle();
rectangle.fill = {
  type: constants.FillType.color,
  color: colorUtils.fromHex("#0066CC")
};

editor.context.insertionParent.children.append(rectangle);

// Use viewport to navigate to the new content
viewport.bringIntoView(rectangle);
`}</code></pre>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "common-mistakes"
    }}>{`Common Mistakes`}</h3>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-javascript"
      }}>{`// ❌ Mistake 1: Trying to import TypeScript classes
import { Editor } from "express-document-sdk";
const myEditor = new Editor();  // Error! Cannot instantiate

// ❌ Mistake 2: Wrong import style for the Express Document SDK
import express from "express-document-sdk";
const { editor } = express;  // Wrong! Use named imports

// ✅ Correct: Import the singleton instances directly
import { editor, colorUtils } from "express-document-sdk";
editor.createRectangle();
colorUtils.fromHex("#FF0000");
`}</code></pre>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "add-on-development-best-practices"
    }}>{`Add-on Development Best Practices`}</h2>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "1-clear-file-organization"
    }}>{`1. Clear File Organization`}</h3>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Keep UI logic in `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`index.js`}</inlineCode></li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Keep document sandbox logic in `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`code.js`}</inlineCode></li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Use consistent naming for exposed APIs`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Separate concerns clearly between environments`}</li>
    </ul>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "2-error-handling"
    }}>{`2. Error Handling`}</h3>
    <p>{`Always handle errors gracefully in both environments:`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-js"
      }}>{`/**
 * BEST PRACTICE: Error Handling
 * APPLIES TO: Both iframe runtime and document sandbox
 * WHY: Graceful failures improve user experience and aid debugging
 */

// Iframe Runtime: Wait for SDK ready state
addOnUISdk.ready.then(() => {
  const { runtime } = addOnUISdk.instance;
  // Safe to use runtime now
}).catch(error => {
  console.error("SDK initialization failed:", error);
});

// Document Sandbox: Wrap API calls in try-catch
runtime.exposeApi({
  createText: function(text) {
    try {
      // Direct document editing
      const textNode = editor.createText(text);
      editor.context.insertionParent.children.append(textNode);
      return { success: true };
    } catch (error) {
      // Log error for debugging
      console.error("Text creation failed:", error.message);
      // Return error info to caller
      return { success: false, error: error.message };
    }
  }
});
`}</code></pre>
    <InlineAlert variant="info" slots="header,text1" mdxType="InlineAlert" />
    <p>{`Best Practices Summary`}</p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Always wrap API calls in try-catch blocks`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Provide meaningful error messages to users`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Handle communication failures gracefully`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Use `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`console.error()`}</inlineCode>{` for debugging in sandbox`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Validate inputs before processing`}</li>
    </ul>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "3-performance"
    }}>{`3. Performance`}</h3>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Minimize data transfer between environments`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Batch multiple operations when possible`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Use appropriate data types (see `}<a parentName="li" {...{
          "href": "../../../references/document-sandbox/communication/index.md#data-types"
        }}>{`Communication APIs`}</a>{`)`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Avoid frequent cross-runtime communication in loops`}</li>
    </ul>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "4-debugging"
    }}>{`4. Debugging`}</h3>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Use `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`console.log`}</inlineCode>{` in both environments`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Check browser dev tools for iframe runtime`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Use sandbox console for document sandbox debugging`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Use `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`console.assert()`}</inlineCode>{` for validation checks`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Test communication flows thoroughly`}</li>
    </ul>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "5-security"
    }}>{`5. Security`}</h3>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Never expose sensitive data through communication APIs`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Validate all data received from the UI`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Use the sandbox's isolation as intended`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Don't attempt to bypass security restrictions`}</li>
    </ul>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "faqs"
    }}>{`FAQs`}</h2>
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "q-why-are-there-two-different-runtime-objects"
    }}>{`Q: Why are there two different runtime objects?`}</h4>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`A:`}</strong>{` Each environment has its own runtime object that acts as a "communication phone." The `}<strong parentName="p">{`iframe runtime`}</strong>{` calls the document sandbox, and the `}<strong parentName="p">{`document sandbox`}</strong>{` calls the iframe runtime. This separation ensures security and proper isolation.`}</p>
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "q-when-do-i-use-addonuisdkinstanceruntime-vs-addonsandboxsdkinstanceruntime"
    }}>{`Q: When do I use `}<inlineCode parentName="h4">{`addOnUISdk.instance.runtime`}</inlineCode>{` vs `}<inlineCode parentName="h4">{`addOnSandboxSdk.instance.runtime`}</inlineCode>{`?`}</h4>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`A:`}</strong>{` Use the runtime object from the environment you're currently in:`}</p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul">{`In `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`index.js`}</inlineCode>{` (iframe runtime): Use `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`addOnUISdk.instance.runtime`}</inlineCode></li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`In `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`code.js`}</inlineCode>{` (document sandbox): Use `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`addOnSandboxSdk.instance.runtime`}</inlineCode></li>
    </ul>
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "q-what-does-await-runtimeapiproxydocumentsandbox-actually-do"
    }}>{`Q: What does `}<inlineCode parentName="h4">{`await runtime.apiProxy("documentSandbox")`}</inlineCode>{` actually do?`}</h4>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`A:`}</strong>{` It creates a proxy object that lets you call functions you've exposed in the document sandbox from your iframe runtime code. Think of it as getting a "remote control" for the other environment.`}</p>
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "q-whats-the-difference-between-documentsandbox-and-panel-in-apiproxy"
    }}>{`Q: What's the difference between `}<inlineCode parentName="h4">{`"documentSandbox"`}</inlineCode>{` and `}<inlineCode parentName="h4">{`"panel"`}</inlineCode>{` in apiProxy?`}</h4>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`A:`}</strong>{` These specify which environment you want to communicate with:`}</p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul"><inlineCode parentName="li">{`"documentSandbox"`}</inlineCode>{` - Call from iframe runtime to document sandbox`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul"><inlineCode parentName="li">{`"panel"`}</inlineCode>{` - Call from document sandbox to iframe runtime`}</li>
    </ul>
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "q-can-i-access-document-apis-directly-from-the-iframe-runtime"
    }}>{`Q: Can I access Document APIs directly from the iframe runtime?`}</h4>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`A:`}</strong>{` No, Document APIs (Express Document SDK) are only available in the document sandbox for security reasons. You must use the communication system (Document Sandbox SDK) to bridge between environments.`}</p>
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "q-how-do-i-know-which-environment-my-code-is-running-in"
    }}>{`Q: How do I know which environment my code is running in?`}</h4>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`A:`}</strong>{` Check your file structure and imports:`}</p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul">{`If you're importing `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`addOnUISdk`}</inlineCode>{` (Add-on UI SDK) → You're in the iframe runtime`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`If you're importing `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`addOnSandboxSdk`}</inlineCode>{` (Document Sandbox SDK) → You're in the document sandbox`}</li>
    </ul>
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "q-do-i-always-need-both-imports-in-my-codejs-file"
    }}>{`Q: Do I always need both imports in my code.js file?`}</h4>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`A:`}</strong>{` No! It depends on what your add-on does:`}</p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li">{`Communication only`}</strong>{`: Just Document Sandbox SDK (`}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`addOnSandboxSdk`}</inlineCode>{`) - processing data without document changes`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li">{`Communication AND document manipulation`}</strong>{`: Both SDKs (Document Sandbox SDK + Express Document SDK) - most common pattern`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`You typically need both if you're creating/modifying document content based on UI interactions`}</li>
    </ul>
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "q-when-does-my-document-sandbox-code-codejs-actually-execute"
    }}>{`Q: When does my document sandbox code (code.js) actually execute?`}</h4>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`A:`}</strong>{` Your `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`code.js`}</inlineCode>{` only executes when your add-on's panel is opened by the user. Specifically:`}</p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li">{`If you have a panel entrypoint with `}<inlineCode parentName="strong">{`documentSandbox: "code.js"`}</inlineCode></strong>{` in your manifest, the code.js file loads when the user opens your add-on panel`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`The code doesn't run automatically on document load or in the background`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`It only executes in the context of your add-on being actively used`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`This ensures security and performance by only running code when needed`}</li>
    </ul>
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "q-what-else-is-available-in-the-document-sandbox-sdk-package"
    }}>{`Q: What else is available in the Document Sandbox SDK package?`}</h4>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`A:`}</strong>{` Besides `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`runtime`}</inlineCode>{`, the Document Sandbox SDK provides:`}</p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li">{`Web APIs`}</strong>{`: Limited browser APIs like `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`console`}</inlineCode>{` and `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`Blob`}</inlineCode>{` for debugging and data handling`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li">{`Automatic global injections`}</strong>{`: No need to import basic APIs like `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`console`}</inlineCode></li>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li">{`Secure execution environment`}</strong>{`: Isolated JavaScript context with performance monitoring`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li">{`Communication infrastructure`}</strong>{`: Handles the complex proxy system between environments`}</li>
    </ul>
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "q-can-i-use-fetch-or-other-network-apis-in-the-document-sandbox"
    }}>{`Q: Can I use fetch() or other network APIs in the document sandbox?`}</h4>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`A:`}</strong>{` No, network APIs like `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`fetch()`}</inlineCode>{` are not available in the document sandbox for security reasons. If you need to make network requests, do them in the iframe runtime and pass the data to the document sandbox via communication APIs (Document Sandbox SDK).`}</p>
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "q-can-my-codejs-file-run-without-a-ui-panel"
    }}>{`Q: Can my code.js file run without a UI panel?`}</h4>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`A:`}</strong>{` No, for third-party add-ons, the document sandbox (`}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`code.js`}</inlineCode>{`) only runs in the context of a panel entrypoint. Your add-on must have a UI (panel) that users interact with, and the document sandbox code executes to support that UI's document manipulation needs.`}</p>
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "q-why-does-my-add-on-feel-slower-in-the-document-sandbox"
    }}>{`Q: Why does my add-on feel slower in the document sandbox?`}</h4>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`A:`}</strong>{` The document sandbox runs in an isolated environment (QuickJS) which is inherently slower than the native browser JavaScript engine. This is by design for security. Minimize complex operations and data transfer between environments.`}</p>
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "q-what-is-the-singleton-pattern-and-which-sdks-use-it"
    }}>{`Q: What is the singleton pattern and which SDKs use it?`}</h4>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`A:`}</strong>{` `}<strong parentName="p">{`All three`}</strong>{` Adobe Express add-on SDKs use the `}<strong parentName="p">{`singleton pattern`}</strong>{`:`}</p>
    <ol>
      <li parentName="ol"><strong parentName="li">{`Add-on UI SDK`}</strong>{` (`}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`addOnUISdk`}</inlineCode>{`) - Pre-instantiated SDK with `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`.instance`}</inlineCode>{` and `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`.app`}</inlineCode>{` properties`}</li>
      <li parentName="ol"><strong parentName="li">{`Document Sandbox SDK`}</strong>{` (`}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`addOnSandboxSdk`}</inlineCode>{`) - Pre-instantiated SDK with `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`.instance`}</inlineCode>{` property`}</li>
      <li parentName="ol"><strong parentName="li">{`Express Document SDK`}</strong>{` - Multiple singleton exports: `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`editor`}</inlineCode>{`, `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`colorUtils`}</inlineCode>{`, `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`constants`}</inlineCode>{`, `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`fonts`}</inlineCode>{`, `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`viewport`}</inlineCode></li>
    </ol>
    <p>{`These are pre-instantiated objects you import and use directly. You `}<strong parentName="p">{`never create new instances yourself`}</strong>{` (no `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`new`}</inlineCode>{` keyword). This ensures:`}</p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul">{`All your code works with the same SDK instances`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Consistent state across your add-on`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`No conflicts from multiple instances`}</li>
    </ul>
    <p>{`See the `}<a parentName="p" {...{
        "href": "#sdk-structure--import-patterns"
      }}>{`SDK Structure & Import Patterns`}</a>{` section for complete details and common mistakes to avoid.`}</p>
